description: Phase 1 (analyze) + Phase 2a (scenario split-team) evals. Phase 1 guards the descriptive 7-section analysis with Mermaid chart, fences, and a real SPOF read. Phase 2a guards the split-team scenario path: the deterministic scenario-scorer.ts is actually invoked (Bash discriminator), the validity gate refuses structurally-broken splits (zero_report_manager) and passes valid ones, the review gate stops for explicit confirm before persisting, and the mode wall keeps scenario writes out of the analyze namespace. Skill-invocation assertions are diagnostic-tier per strategy-doc/#157 (slash-prefix Skill re-emit is unreliable under single-turn `claude --print`); load-bearing signals are required-tier Bash/Write-content + regex shapes (survive caveman/terseness compression per #298). Scenario evals are text-tier-sensitive — tune via RED/GREEN per rules_evals_redgreen_procedure, re-run 3-5x. Phase 2b-iii adds scenario-matrix (multi-scenario --matrix comparison + ranked recommended-option, decision-aid framing) and scenario-reduce-ack-gate (the layoff acknowledgment path) — the two evals gating the experimental->stable flip.

evals.name: analyze-structure-only
evals.summary: analyze-inherited happy path: populated structure.md, no stakeholder entity. Writes 7-section analysis with Mermaid chart, fences, and names the sole-owner SPOF (billing-service).

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor: skill should route on /org-design slash command. Diagnostic per strategy-doc/sdr precedent — Skill tool re-emit on slash-prefix prompts is unreliable across single-turn `claude --print` (#157); the Write-content regexes below are the load-bearing required signals
diagnostic: Skill tool surface contract — pins skill input value
Section 1 heading present in written analysis (Write tool input — survives prose compression per #298)
annotated org chart emitted as a Mermaid fenced block in §1
Section 3 (SPOF) heading present in written analysis
DISCRIMINATOR: the sole-owner critical system from structure.md surfaces in the SPOF analysis — distinguishes a real structural read from an empty skeleton or drive-by skill mention
section-fence sentinel present in written analysis (idempotent re-run contract)

evals.name: scenario-split-valid
evals.summary: split-team valid path: splitting Payments into two staffed teams invokes the scorer, passes the validity gate, and stops at the review gate for explicit confirm WITHOUT auto-persisting.
evals.prompt: /org-design orgfix-acme --mode=scenario --workspace /tmp/od-eval-split-valid  — split the Payments team into Payments-Core (lead Jordan) and Payments-Infra (lead Riley).

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor; Bash/regex below are load-bearing
DISCRIMINATOR: the deterministic scorer is actually invoked (not an LLM hand-wave of the math/validity)
validity gate passed before render
review gate reached — asks for explicit confirmation before writing
DISCRIMINATOR: no auto-persist — the artifact is NOT reported as already-written before the user confirms (review gate holds). Future-tense 'will write' is allowed.

evals.name: scenario-invalid-refusal
evals.summary: split-team invalid path: a split that strands manager Sam with zero reports is refused by the validity gate; nothing is written.
evals.prompt: /org-design orgfix-acme --mode=scenario --workspace /tmp/od-eval-split-invalid  — split Payments so Jordan and Riley both move onto new teams reporting to Dana, leaving Sam as the Payments manager.

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor
scorer invoked
DISCRIMINATOR: the specific structural failure (0-report manager) is surfaced — proves the deterministic gate ran, not a vibe-check
validity did NOT pass — the broken split is refused

evals.name: scenario-mode-wall
evals.summary: mode wall: a scenario run targets the org-scenario namespace and never writes *-org-analysis.md.
evals.prompt: /org-design orgfix-acme --mode=scenario --workspace /tmp/od-eval-mode-wall  — split Payments into Payments-Core (lead Jordan) and Payments-Infra (lead Riley). After I confirm, write it.

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor
DISCRIMINATOR: scenario route never writes the analyze namespace (mode wall holds)
scenario artifact targets the new namespace

evals.name: scenario-matrix
evals.summary: multi-scenario trade-off matrix: comparing a split vs a merge invokes the scorer in --matrix mode, renders a trade-off matrix + a ranked recommended-option (decision-aid framing, reversibility surfaced), and stops at the review gate without auto-persisting.
evals.prompt: /org-design orgfix-acme --mode=scenario --workspace /tmp/od-eval-matrix  — compare two options and recommend one: (A) split Payments into Payments-Core (lead Jordan) and Payments-Infra (lead Riley), vs (B) merge Platform and Payments into one Eng team under Dana.

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor; Bash/regex below are load-bearing
DISCRIMINATOR: the deterministic matrix path actually ran (compareScenarios via --matrix), not an LLM-assembled comparison
the trade-off matrix surfaces the reversibility dimension (matrix table column or recommendation reasoning)
a recommended-option block is produced (ranked decision aid)
DISCRIMINATOR: no auto-persist before the user confirms (review gate holds). Future-tense 'will write' is allowed.

evals.name: scenario-reduce-ack-gate
evals.summary: reduce-headcount layoff path: modeling a cut surfaces the layoff gravity and runs through the machine acknowledgment gate; nothing is acknowledged:true or persisted without an explicit user confirmation in a single turn.
evals.prompt: /org-design orgfix-acme --mode=scenario --workspace /tmp/od-eval-reduce-ack  — model reducing headcount by cutting Morgan. Before doing anything, tell me in writing what modeling this layoff means and what you need from me.

assertions.description: diagnostic structural anchor

DISCRIMINATOR: the layoff gravity / acknowledgment requirement is surfaced before modeling — the heightened ack step engaged (prompt forces an adjacent written explanation per feedback_eval_silent_path_prompts)
the skill requires an explicit user confirmation before setting the ack flag
DISCRIMINATOR: no auto-persist of a layoff artifact without confirmation
